summary: Moved the common Yoshi egg action out of ft_08A4 assembly into a new C translation unit, added it to the build, and introduced the motion-variable and common-attribute typing needed to express the code. The new ftCo_YoshiEgg.c report was 98.81% matched overall: all listed routines except ftCo_800BBED4 were 100%, while ftCo_800BBED4 remained partial at 96.79%. The PR also converted a large amount of ftcoll.s into src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c, tightened many fighter callback prototypes to ftCo_GObj/HSD_GObjEvent forms, adjusted supporting headers, added numeric constants to macros.inc, and retargeted convert_data.py toward ftcoll.s. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_YoshiEgg.c and .h and registered the new C object in obj_files.mk before the remaining asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o.

Removed the Yoshi egg routines from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and reimplemented/exported ftCo_800BBC88, ftCo_800BBCC0, ftCo_800BBED4, ftCo_YoshiEgg_Anim, ftCo_YoshiEgg_IASA, ftCo_YoshiEgg_Phys, ftCo_YoshiEgg_Coll, ftCo_800BC388, ftCo_800BC3AC, and ftCo_800BC3D0 in C or guarded ASM C stubs.
Introduced ftCommon motion variables for mv.co.yoshiegg at fp+2340 through fp+236F, including the captured Yoshi GObj pointer, egg timing/rate fields, original joint scale, and accessory scale.

Added a typed ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t structure for the common-attribute block at co_attrs.xBC, replacing several raw int fields with size, two Vec3s, and a float; updated Yoshi guard/init and Yoshi egg effect code to pass that typed block.

Converted or partially converted substantial ft collision code: asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s lost 1105 lines while src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c gained many functions, with the report showing many ftColl_80076xxx/8007xxxx routines now 100% matched and others still unmatched or partial.

Cleaned up callback signatures across common and character files by removing unused float lag parameters and representing callback parameters as HSD_GObjEvent where appropriate, notably ft_80082C74 and many landing/coll callback helpers.
Updated ft_08A4.h from a mostly broad declaration header toward ftCo_GObj-specific prototypes and added declarations for nearby ftCo_800BC438/800BC458/800BC4A8/800C0874/800C0A28/800C0C88 symbols.
Added assembler constants U8_MAX, U16_MAX, U32_MAX, S8_MAX, S16_MAX, S32_MAX, and F32_MAX to src/macros.inc; normalized platform.h F32_MAX literal casing from E to e.
Adjusted src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h HSD_ASSERT line-number literals from hex to decimal in several inline JObj setters, matching the generated/asserted source form used by the decompiled code.
Changed tools/convert_data.py defaults from ftaction.s to ftcoll.s and widened accepted s32/u32 value ranges from +/-1000 to +/-5000 for ftcoll data conversion work.
smart_moves: Kept the Yoshi egg work isolated in a dedicated ftCo_YoshiEgg.c/.h while leaving the surrounding ft_08A4 assembly object in the build, allowing incremental replacement rather than forcing the whole ft_08A4 unit to match at once.

Used the existing common fighter callback fields directly: the egg setup assigns fp->cb.x21BC_callback_Accessory4 to ftCo_800BBCC0 and fp->cb.x21F0_callback to ftCo_800BC3D0, which makes the decompiled control flow mirror the original callback-driven behavior.

Modeled the egg scaling logic with Vec3 temporaries and HSD_JObjSetScale/HSD_JObjGetScale instead of raw offset writes where possible, clarifying that the routine interpolates model/accessory scale during egg growth or shrink.

Preserved nonmatching sections under MUST_MATCH/WIP guarded ASM blocks, especially around ftCo_800BBCC0, ftCo_800BBED4, ftCo_YoshiEgg_Anim, and ftColl_8007B62C, so progress could land without regressing matchability.
Promoted raw common-attribute xBC data into ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t and reused it in effect-spawn callsites; this made Yoshi shield/egg effect code type-aware instead of scattering raw int pointers.

Standardized callback prototypes broadly in the same PR, reducing the number of misleading dummy float lag parameters and making helper APIs such as ft_80082C74 align with HSD_GObjEvent callback conventions.
decomp_lessons: For common action files, add a small dedicated C file and object entry before the old aggregate assembly object; this lets individual action states be matched while the rest of the address range remains in assembly.

When motion variables occupy fp+2340 scratch space, give the union member an action-specific name and annotate offsets. The yoshiegg member documents fp+2340, fp+2344, fp+2348, fp+234C, fp+2350, fp+2354, fp+2358, and fp+2364 usage and makes later action code safer.

If a callback function is passed through a generic event slot, prefer the project callback typedef such as HSD_GObjEvent over ad hoc function-pointer signatures; remove phantom parameters if callsites do not supply them.

Effect-spawn and attribute-pointer callsites often reveal hidden substructures. The xBC common-attribute block was better represented as ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t once multiple Yoshi-related callsites used it as a structured effect parameter.

Small support changes can unblock matching: assembler constants in macros.inc, exact float max literal formatting, and decimal-vs-hex assert line numbers in inline baselib functions can affect generated assembly or source-derived asserts.

Landing many 100% matched small routines alongside a partially matched larger routine is acceptable when guarded ASM fallback preserves correctness and the report clearly shows the remaining score debt.
naming_conventions: New common action files follow ftCo_ActionName.c/.h, here ftCo_YoshiEgg.c and ftCo_YoshiEgg.h.
Common action callbacks use ftCo_YoshiEgg_Anim, ftCo_YoshiEgg_IASA, ftCo_YoshiEgg_Phys, and ftCo_YoshiEgg_Coll for the standard animation/interrupt/physics/collision state hooks.
Unknown but address-stable functions remain in ftCo_800xxxxx or ftColl_800xxxxx form, with address comments in headers, rather than inventing semantic names not supported by evidence.
Common fighter GObj parameters are increasingly typed as ftCo_GObj* or Fighter_GObj* rather than raw HSD_GObj* when the function belongs to fighter/common code.
Action-specific motion variables use mv.co.yoshiegg with short field names x0/x4/x8/xC/x10/x14/x18/scale and offset comments, consistent with partially known structs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the original ftCo_YoshiEgg assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced it with a new C object while keeping remaining ft_08A4 assembly available.

Used `ASM void` functions with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies and an `#else` raw assembly fallback for routines that were understood but not fully matched under strict conditions.
Introduced `#pragma force_active on` under WIP in ftCo_YoshiEgg.c, suggesting data/function retention was needed while iterating on matching.

Preserved exact constants and callbacks such as ftCo_MS_YoshiEgg, Ft_MF_Unk06, Ft_MF_KeepGfx, ftCommon_SetAccessory, ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDD4, ftYs_Shield_8012CC94/CCC4/CCD0/CCF0/CCE0/CD00, and ftColl_8007B62C rather than abstracting them away.
Adjusted convert_data.py to scan ftcoll.s and broaden integer detection ranges, supporting the large ftcoll.s to ftcoll.c conversion in the same slice.
The matching report indicates the new YoshiEgg file was nearly complete: 9 of 10 listed routines scored 100%, and the only remaining nonzero score was ftCo_800BBED4 at 587/18300.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched ftCo_800BBED4 or removed the guarded ASM from ftCo_YoshiEgg.c.
Find later renames of ftCo_800BBC88, ftCo_800BBCC0, ftCo_800BC388, ftCo_800BC3AC, and ftCo_800BC3D0.
Search for additional users or refinements of ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t and co_attrs.xBC.
Search for PRs continuing the ftcoll.c conversion after ftcoll.s lost 1105 lines in this PR.
Search for follow-up cleanup of ft_08A4.h prototypes introduced or retouched by this PR.
